steps: Tom needs a mean score of at least 90 across four exams. The mean of four values equals their sum divided by 4.
The three known scores are 85, 78, and 98. Let $G$ be the fourth. The mean is $\frac{G + 85 + 78 + 98}{4}$.
"At least 90" means $\geq 90$, so the inequality is $\frac{G + 85 + 78 + 98}{4} \geq 90$.

• A: $-(85+78+98) \leq 4G$ — Negating the known scores is mathematically unrelated to computing a mean. Eliminated.
  • B: $G + 85 + 78 + 98 \geq 360$ — Equivalent to choice C after multiplying both sides by 4 (since $4 \times 90 = 360$), but this expression does not represent the mean directly as the question asks. Eliminated.
  • C: $\frac{G + 85 + 78 + 98}{4} \geq 90$ — Directly expresses "mean of all four exams is at least 90." That matches choice C — C is correct.
  • D: $\frac{85+78+98}{4} \geq 90 - 4G$ — Divides only three scores by 4 and moves $G$ incorrectly. Eliminated.
